query: South Korea KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell value weekly latest June 2026, recent trend in trillion won
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on KOSPI foreign investor flows:

**Bottom line:** Foreign investors sold KOSPI stock at a record pace in H1 2026 (~148.3 trillion won Jan–Jun, ~157.3T through July 3) even as the index surged ~92% YTD, but they've made a **tentative return to net buying in early-mid July**.

**Most recent weekly/daily flows (the key data requested):**
- **July 8:** +343.6 billion won (net buy)
- **July 9:** +134.8 billion won (net buy)
- Combined ~0.48 trillion won — first two-day buying streak since mid-June, following **13 straight sessions of selling (June 19–July 7)**

**Recent trend arc (in trillion won):**
- June 12: turned net buyers for first time in 25 sessions (~2T in one day)
- June 12–16: ~4.82T net buying
- June 19–July 7: sustained net selling (13 sessions)
- July 8–9: modest net buying resumes (~0.48T)

**Context:** ~90% of foreign selling was Samsung Electronics + SK Hynix; drivers include won weakness (~1,535/USD), profit-taking after the ~100% rally, and dollar strength. The KOSPI fell ~13% over the past month from a June 19 intraday peak near 9,385 to close 7,435 on July 13. Brokerages expect foreign selling may continue into H2 2026.

Full structured findings (key facts, 12 data points, and 11 sources) have been delivered via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: Foreign investors' record Kospi sell-off may continue in second half as won weakens
sources.snippet: A record 148 trillion won ($95 billion) in net selling by foreign investors during the first half; net sales of 148.32 trillion won in the Kospi market Jan-Jun.

sources.title: Foreign investors may have more Korean stocks to sell
sources.snippet: Foreign investors sold 157.3 trillion won ($102.5 billion) on the KOSPI through July 3; index surged 91.9%; ownership rose to 40.47%; retail net bought 104.8T.

sources.title: Foreign Investors Return, Fueling KOSPI Rebound Hopes
sources.snippet: July 8 +343.6bn won, July 9 +134.8bn won net buying; prior 13-session selling streak Jun 19-Jul 7; June 12-16 buying of 4.82 trillion won.

sources.title: Foreign Investors Dump 150 Trillion Won in KOSPI in First Half
sources.snippet: KOSPI closed at 8,476.48 on June 30; foreign investors dumped nearly 150 trillion won in the first half.

sources.title: Foreign Buying Lifts Kospi Back Above 8,000; Volatility to Persist Next Week
sources.snippet: Foreigners returned after 35 days, net buyers of about 2 trillion won led by Samsung and SK Hynix and KOSPI 200 futures; Kospi +4.63%.

sources.title: Foreigners Dump $106.8 Billion of Kospi Stocks in First Half as Samsung Ownership Hits 16-Year Low
sources.snippet: Foreign investors net sellers of 148.316 trillion won on the Kospi in H1, concentrated in Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix.

sources.title: Kospi, SK Hynix, Samsung Electronics: Why foreign investors are selling
sources.snippet: On Monday overseas investors unloaded a net 1.24 trillion won (~$801 million) of Kospi-listed shares per Korea Exchange data.

sources.title: Foreign Investors Absorb Retail Selloff as KOSPI Sees 1 Trillion Won Net Buying
sources.snippet: KOSPI reclaimed 8,500 as foreigners net bought ~1 trillion won absorbing retail selloff on June 15.

sources.title: Investor Cash Reserves Fall Below 110 Trillion Won as Retail Buyers Exhausted
sources.snippet: Investor deposits fell below 110 trillion won for first time in five months amid a sharp KOSPI sell-off threatening the 7,000 level.

sources.title: Kospi jumps 2.52% as chip stocks rally; Korean won strengthens
sources.snippet: Seoul shares closed higher July 10 as semiconductor gains lifted the benchmark for a second straight session; won strengthened.

sources.title: KOSPI Composite Index (^KS11) historical prices
sources.snippet: KOSPI intraday peak near 9,385 on June 19; closes: 7,246.79 (Jul 8), 7,291.91 (Jul 9), 7,475.94 (Jul 10), 7,435.40 (Jul 13).
